Original from
UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
OF THE
COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
PASSED AT THE EXTRAORDINARY SESSION OF THE GENERAL
ASSEMBLY, WHICH WAS BEGUN IN THE CITY OF FRANKFORT,
KENTUCKY, ON MONDAY, MARCH THE FIFTH, 1951, AND ENDED
WEDNESDAY, MARCH THE FOURTEENTH, 1951.
CHAPTER 1
(H. B. l)
AN ACT appropriating money out of the General Expenditure Fund for
the fiscal year ending June 30, 1951, for expenses of sessions of the
General Assembly.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common
wealth of Kentucky:
Sec. 1. There is hereby appropriated out of the fund in the
General Expenditure Fund not otherwise appropriated for the
fiscal year ending June 30, 1951, the sum of $100,000 for expenses
of sessions of the General Assembly.
Sec. 2. Whereas there is no appropriation made for the ex
penses of Legislative Sessions for the fiscal year ending June 30,
1951, and it is necessary that funds be immediately provided
for the Extraordinary Session of the General Assembly of March,
1951, an emergency is declared to exist and this Act shall take
effect from and after its passage and approval by the Governor.
Approved March 13, 1951.

UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA

CHAPTER 2
(H. B. 2)
AN ACT relating to appropriations for the Department of Economic
Security for public assistance benefits and their administration; for
the Department of Welfare for certain designated public institutions
and for administrative expenses of said department; for the Depart
ment of Education for the Common School Per Capita Fund, and
declaring an emergency with respect to certain of said appropria
tions.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common
wealth of Kentucky:
PART I
There are hereby appropriated to the Department of Wel
fare out of any presently existing surplus in the General Expendi
ture Fund the following sums for the fiscal year ending June 30,
1951, which shall be in addition to any appropriation heretofore
made for the said fiscal year:
A. Penal Institutions. For expenses of the penal institu
tions; houses of reform, State Reformatory, State Penitentiary and
Women's Prison $150,000.00
B. Office of the Commissioner. For expenses of administra
tion of the Department of Welfare $5,000.00
C. Mental Hospitals. For expenses of the mental institu
tions; Eastern State Hospital, Central State Hospital, Western
State Hospital, Kentucky State Hospital and The Kentucky
Training Home $95,000.00
PART II
There are hereby appropriated out of any surplus existing in
the General Expenditure Fund as of June 30, 1951, for the fiscal
year beginning on July 1, 1951, and ending on June 30, 1952, the
following sums for the following departments, or the designated
Division thereof, which sum shall be in addition to any appro
priation hereofore made for said fiscal year:

8 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
SECTION 1. DEPARTMENT OF EDUCATION.
To the Department of Education for the Common School
Per Capita Fund, the sum of $6,000,000.00, for the purpose of
increasing the salaries of teachers employed in the public ele
mentary and secondary schools of Kentucky for the fiscal year
beginning July 1, 1951, and ending June 30, 1952. The sum so
appropriated shall be distributed to each school district by the
Superintendent of Public Instruction as a part of and in addition
to the Common School Per Capita Fund appropriated by the
Regular Session of the 1950 General Assembly, the distribution
to be in accordance with the law governing the distribution of
the Common School Per Capita Fund.
SECTION 2. DEPARTMENT OF ECONOMIC SE
CURITY.
To the Department of Economic Security for ordinary ex
penses of operation and cooperation (matching of funds) with
the Federal Government under the Social Security Act of August
14, 1935, and any amendments now in effect or hereafter enacted
and under any act of Congress supplementing any amendment,
including adjustments of prior year or current year accounts
necessary to comply with the Federal regulations and for pur
poses of administration:
Public Assistance— Benefits and Costs of Administration. For
Old Age Assistance payments, Aid to the Needy Blind and Aid to
Dependent Children, and not more than 5% of which shall be
used for cost of administration $2,000,000.00
SECTION 3. DEPARTMENT OF WELFARE.
To the Department of Welfare the following sums:
A. Office of the Commissioner. For expenses of the ad
ministration of the Department of Welfare $20,000.00
B. Mental Hospitals. For expenses of the mental institu
tions; Eastern State Hospital, Central State Hospital, Western
State Hospital and Kentucky State Hospital and The Kentucky
Training Home __$450,000.00

CHAPTER 2 9
C. Penal Institutions. For expenses of the penal institu
tions; houses of reform, State Reformatory, State Penitentiary
and the Women's Prison $250,000.00
D. Kentucky Children's Home $30,000.00
PART III
WHEREAS, the inflationary economic trend has greatly in
creased the cost of supporting, maintaining and treating inmates
or wards in the mental, penal and other institutions of the Com
monwealth and has greatly increased the administrative ex
penses of the Office of the Commissioner of the Department of
Welfare; and,
WHEREAS, the budgetary allotment to the Department of
Welfare for the fiscal year 1950-1951 is insufficient to enable the
Department of Welfare to adequately care for the inmates or
wards of its mental, penal and other institutions and is insuffi
cient to defray the administrative expenses of the Commissioner
of the Department of Welfare; and
NOW, THEREFORE, an emergency is declared to exist as
to the appropriations made under Part I of this act and as to such
appropriations this act shall become effective upon its passage
and approval by the Governor.
The appropriations made under Part II of this act shall
become effective on July 1, 1951.
Approved March 14, 1951.

UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA

CHAPTER 3
(S. B. 1)
AN ACT to provide for the coverage of certain officers and employes of
the state and local governments, the political subdivisions and
agencies thereof, and interstate instrumentalities, and the dependents
and survivors of such officers and employes, under the Old-Age and
Survivors Insurance provisions of Title II of the Federal Social Se
curity Act, as amended, making an appropriation therefor, and de
claring an emergency to exist.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common
wealth of Kentucky:
Sec. 1. Declaration of Policy. In order to extend to cer
tain employes of the Commonwealth and its political subdivi
sions, and interstate instrumentalities, not now members of
public retirement systems, and to the dependents and survivors
of such employes, the basic protection accorded to others by the
Old-Age and Survivors Insurance System embodied in the Social
Security Act, it is hereby declared to be the policy of the Gen-
eral Assembly to provide such protection to such employes, and
their dependents and survivors, on as broad a basis as is per
mitted under the Social Security Act.
Sec. 2. Definitions. For the purposes of this Act.
(a) The term "wages" means all remuneration for employ
ment as defined herein, including the cash value of all re
muneration paid in any medium other than1 cash, except that
such term shall not include that part of such remuneration which,
even if it were for "employment" within the meaning of the
Federal Insurance Contributions Act, would not constitute
"wages" within the meaning of that Act.
(b) The term "employment" means any service performed
by an employe in the employ of the Commonwealth, a political
subdivision, or an interstate instrumentality, for such employer,
except (1) service of an emergency nature, (2) service which in
the absence of an agreement entered into under this Act would
constitute "employment" as defined in the Social Security Act, or

12 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
(3) service which under the Social Security Act may not be in
cluded in any agreement between the Commonwealth and Fed
eral Security Administrator entered into under tihs Act;
Provided, however, that service, the compensation for which
is on a fee basis, may be excluded in any plan approved under
this Act, and provided also, that service in any class or classes of
positions, the exclusion of which is permitted under the Social
Security Act, may be excluded in any plan approved under
Section 6 of this Act.
(c) The term "employe," in addition to its usual meaning,
includes both elective and appointive officers of the Common
wealth, political subdivisions, or interstate instrumentalities.
(d) The term "State Agency" means the Division of Per
sonnel Security in the Department of Economic Security.
(e) The term "Federal Security Administrator" includes any
individual to whom the Federal Security Administrator has dele
gated any of his functions under the Social Security Act with
respect to coverage under such act of employes of States and
their political subdivisions.
(f) The term "political subdivision," in addition to coun
ties, municipal corporations and school districts, includes instru
mentalities of the Commonwealth, of one or more of its political
subdivisions, or of the Commonwealth and one or more of its
political subdivisions, and any other governmental unit thereof.
(g) The term "Social Security Act" means the Act of Con
gress approved August 14, 1935, Chapter 531, '49 Stat. 620,
officially cited as the "Social Security Act," including regulations
and requirements issued pursuant thereto, as such act has been
and may from time to time be amended.
(h) The term "Federal Insurance Contributions Act" means
subchapter A of Chapter 9 of the Federal Internal Revenue
Code as such Code has been and may from time to time be
amended.

CHAPTER 3 13
Sec. 3. Federal-State Agreement. Consistent with the
terms and conditions of this Act, the State Agency, with the
approval of the Governor, is hereby authorized to enter on be
half of the Commonwealth into an agreement with the Federal
Security Administrator, for the purpose of extending the benefits
of the Federal Old-Age and Survivors Insurance System to em
ployes of the Commonwealth or any political subdivision thereof
with respect to services specified in such agreement which con
stitute "employment" as defined in Section 2 of this Act. Such
agreement may contain such provisions relating to coverage,
benefits, contributions, effective date, modification and termina
tion of the agreement, administration and other appropriate pro
visions as the State Agency and Federal Security Administrator
shall agree upon, but except as may be otherwise required by or
under the Social Security Act as to the services to be covered,
such agreement shall provide in effect that:
(a) Benefits will be provided for employes whose services
are covered by the agreement, and their dependents and sur
vivors, on the same basis as though such services constituted
employment within the meaning of Title II of the Social Securitv
Act.
(b) The state will pay to the Secretary of the Treasury, at
such time or times as may be prescribed under the Social Security
Act, contributions with respect to wages equal to the sum of
taxes which would be imposed by Sections 1400 and 1410 of the
Federal Insurance Contributions Act if the services covered by
the agreement constituted employment within the meaning of
that act.
(c) Such agreement shall be effective with respect to serv
ices in employment covered by the agreement performed after
a date specified therein, but in jio event may it be effective with
respect to any such services performed prior to the first day of
the calendar year in which such agreement is entered into or in
which the modification of the agreement making it applicable to
such services is entered into,

14 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
( d) All services which constitute employment and are per
formed in the employ of the Commonwealth by employes of the
Commonwealth shall be covered by the agreement.
•
(e) All services which (1) constitute employment, (2) are
performed in the employ of a political subdivision of the State,
and (3) are covered by a plan which is in conformity with the
terms of the agreement and has been approved by the State
Agency under Section 6, shall be covered by the agreement.
Sec. 4. Interstate Instrumentalities. Any instrumentality
jointly created by this state and any other state or states is
hereby authorized, to the extent that this Commonwealth may
confer authority, (a) to enter into an agreement with the Federal
Security Administrator whereby the benefits of the Federal
Old-Age and Survivors Insurance System shall be extended to
employes of such instrumentality, (b) to require its employes to
pay (and for that purpose to deduct from their wages) contri
butions equal to the amounts which they would be required to
pay under subsection (a) of section 5 of this Act if they were
covered by an agreement made pursuant to section 3 of this Act,
and (c) to make payments to the Secretary of the Treasury in
accordance with such agreement, including payments from its
own funds, and otherwise to comply with such agreement. Such
agreement shall, to the extent practicable, be consistent with the
terms and provisions of section 3, and all other terms and pro
visions, of this Act.
Sec. 5. Contributions by State Employes, (a) Every em
ploye of the state whose services are covered by an agreement
entered into under section 3 shall be required to pay for the
period of such coverage, into the Contribution Fund established
by section 7, contributions, with respect to wages received during
the calendar years 1951 to 1953, both inclusive, equal to 1M per
centum of such wages; with respect to wages received during the
calendar years 1954 to 1959, both inclusive, not to exceed 2 per
centum of such wages; with respect to wages received during the
calendar ears 1960 to 1964, both inclusive, not to exceed 2% per

CHAPTER 3 15
centum of such wages; with respect to wages received during the
calendar years 1965 to 1969, both inclusive, not to exceed 3 per
centum of such wages; and with respect to wages received after
December 31, 1969, not to exceed 3M per centum of such wages.
Such liability shall arise in consideration of the employe's reten
tion in the service of the state, or his entry upon such service,
after the effective date of this Act.
(b) The contribution imposed by this section shall be col
lected by deducting the amount of the contribution from wages
as and when paid, but failure to make such deduction shall not
relieve the employe from liability for such contribution.
(c) If more or less than the correct amount of the contri
bution imposed by this section is paid or deducted with respect
to any remuneration, proper adjustments, or refund if adjustment
is impracticable, shall be made, without interest, in such manner
and at such times as the State Agency shall prescribe.
Sec. 6. Plans for Coverage of Employes of Political Sub
divisions, (a) Each political subdivision of the state is hereby
authorized to submit for approval by the State Agency a plan
for extending the benefits of Title II of the Social Security Act,
in conformity with applicable provisions of such Act, to employes
of such political subdivision. Each such plan and any amend
ment thereof shall be approved by the State Agency if it finds
that such plan, or such plan as amended, is in conformity with
such requirements as are provided in regulations of the State
Agency, except that no such plan shall be approved unless:
( 1 ) It is in conformity with the requirements of the Social
Security Act and with the agreement entered into under Sec
tion 3;
(2) It provides that all services which constitute employ
ment and are performed in the employ of the political subdivision
by employes thereof, shall be covered by the plan;
(3) It specifies the source or sources from which the funds
necessary to make the payments required by paragraph (1) of

16 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
subsection (c) and by subsection (d) are expected to be derived
and contains reasonable assurance that such sources will be
adequate for such purpose;
(4) It provides for such methods of administration of the
plan by the political subdivision as are found by the State Agency
to be necessary for the proper and efficient administration
thereof;
(5) It provides that the political subdivisions will make such
reports, in such form and containing such information, as the
State Agency may from time to time require, and will comply
with such provisions as the State Agency or the Federal Security
Administrator may from time to time find necessary to assure the
correctness and verifications of such reports; and
(6) It authorizes the State Agency to terminate the plan in
its entirety, in the discretion of the State Agency, if it finds that
there has been failure to comply substantially with any provision
contained in such plan, such termination to take effect at the
expiration of such notice and on such conditions as may be
provided by regulations of the State Agency, and as may be
consistent with the provisions of the Social Security Act.
(b) The State Agency shall not finally refuse to approve a
plan submitted by a political subdivision under subsection (a),
and shall not terminate an approved plan, without reasonable
notice and opportunity for hearing to the political subdivision
affected thereby.
(c) (1) Each political subdivision for which a plan has
been approved under this section is authorized to, and shall, pay
into the Contribution Fund, with respect to wages, at such time
or times as the State Agency may by regulation prescribe, con
tributions in the amounts and at the rates specified in the ap
plicable agreement entered into by the State Agency under sec
tion 3, and, furthermore, in anticipation of the due date of any
payments of contributions required herein, is authorized to, and
shall, make such advancements as the State Agency, by regu
lation or contract, may require.

CHAPTER 3 17
(2) Each political subdivision is authorized to, and shall,
make such payments as are determined by the State Agency
to be necessary for the purpose of defraying the expenses in
curred by the State Agency in administering this Act for the
benefit of those employes covered under any plan approved
under Section 6 of this Act, but in no event shall such amount
be greater than 5% of the contributions required under paragraph
(1) of this subsection. Such payments shall be made into the
State Treasury and shall be credited to a separate trust and
agency fund to be used by the State Agency solely for the pur
pose stated herein.
(3) Each political subdivision required to make payments
under paragraph (1) of this subsection is authorized, in con
sideration of the employe's retention in, or entry upon, employ
ment after the effective date of this Act, to impose upon each of
its employes, as to services which are covered by an approved
plan, a contribution, with respect to wages received during the
calendar years 1951 to 1953, both inclusive, equal to 1% per
centum of such wages; with respect to wages received during the
calendar years 1954 to 1959, both inclusive, not to exceed 2 per
centum of such wages; with respect to wages received during the
calendar years 1960 to 1964, both inclusive, not to exceed 2M
per centum of such wages; with respect to wages received during
the calendar years 1965 to 1969, both inclusive, not to exceed 3
per centum of such wages; and with respect to wages received
after December 31, 1969, not to exceed 3/4 per centum of such
wages. Contributions so collected shall be paid into the Con
tribution Fund in partial discharge of the liability of such po
litical subdivision under paragraph ( 1 ) of this subsection. Failure
to deduct such contribution shall not relieve the employe or
employer of liability therefor.
( d ) Delinquent payments due under paragraph ( 1 ) of sub
section (c), with interest at the rate of six per centum per
annum, may be recovered by action in the Franklin Circuit Court
against the political subdivision liable therefor, or may, at the
request of the State Agency, be deducted from any other moneys

18 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
payable to such subdivision by any department or agency of the
state.
Sec. 7. Contribution Fund, (a) There is hereby estab
lished a special fund to be known as the Contribution Fund.
Such fund shall consist of and there shall be deposited therein:
(1) all contributions, interest, and penalties collected under sec
tions 5 and 6; (2) all moneys appropriated or otherwise con
tributed thereto; (3) any property or securities and earnings
thereof acquired through the use of moneys belonging to the
fund; (4) interest earned upon any moneys in the fund, and (5)
all sums recovered from the bond of the custodian or otherwise
for losses sustained by the fund, and all other moneys received
for the fund from any other source. All moneys in the fund shall
be mingled and undivided. Subject to the provisions of this Act,
the State Agency is vested with full power, authority and juris
diction over the fund, including all moneys and property or
securities belonging thereto, and may perform any and all acts
whether or not specifically designated, which are necessary to
the administration thereof and are consistent with the provisions
of this Act.
(b) The Contribution Fund shall be a trust and agency
fund which shall not lapse and shall be held separate and apart
from any other funds or moneys of the state and shall be used
and administered exclusively for the purposes of this Act. With
drawals from such fund shall be made for, and solely for (1)
payment of amounts required to be paid to the Secretary of the
Treasury pursuant to an agreement entered into under section
3; (2) payment of refunds provided for in subsection (c) of
section 5; and (3) refunds of overpayments, not otherwise adjust
able, made by a political subdivision.
(c) From the Contribution Fund the custodian of the fund
shall pay to the Secretary of the Treasury such amounts at such
time or times as may be directed by the State Agency in ac
cordance with any agreement entered into under section 3.
(d) The Treasurer of the state shah1 be ex-officio treasurer
and custodian of the Contribution Fund and shall administer

CHAPTER 3 19
such fund in accordance with the provisions of this Act and the
directions of the State Agency, and shall pay all warrants drawn
upon the fund in accordance with the provisions of this section
and with such regulations as the State Agency may prescribe
pursuant thereto.
Sec. 8. State Contributions Authorized. Authorization is
hereby granted to all offices, departments, boards, commissions,
institutions, and all other agencies of the state government of
the Commonwealth of Kentucky to make payments to the Con
tribution Fund out of moneys, not required by law or contract to
be expended for other purposes, in any revolving, trust or agency
fund, or out of appropriations for recurring expenses heretofore
or hereafter made by the General Assembly from the General
Expenditure Fund or special funds.
Sec. 9. Appropriation. In order to supplement the pay-
merits authorized under Section 8, and to provide assurance that
the obligations arising under Section 3 of this Act will be met,
and in order to provide for the costs of administration, there
is hereby appropriated, to the Governor, out of the General
Expenditure Fund, for the fiscal year 1951-52, the sum of $250,-
000, as a Special Personnel Security Fund to be allocated by the
Governor in his discretion to such budget units or funds, includ
ing the Contribution Fund, as may require additional funds by
reason of this Act.
•
Sec. 10. Rules and Regulations. The State Agency shall
make and publish such rules and regulations, not inconsistent
with the provisions of this Act, as it finds necessary or appro
priate to the efficient administration of the functions with which
it is charged under this Act.
Sec. 11. Division of Personnel Security. There is hereby
created within the Department of Economic Security a Division
of Personnel Security, which shall be headed by a director to be
appointed by the Commissioner of Economic Security, with the
approval of the Governor. Subject to the approval of the Com
missioner of Economic Security, the director is authorized to em

20 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
ploy such assistants, employes, agents and other personnel, and
to take such other action, as may be necessary for the proper
administration of this act. By executive order, the Governor
may transfer this division to some other state department to
more effectively administer the purposes of this act.
Sec. 12. Retroactive Effect of Act. The provisions of this
Act shall be retrospective to January 1, 1951, and any agreement
entered into pursuant to it may be made with retroactive effect
to January 1, 1951, or any date thereafter.
Sec. 13. Emergency Clause. WHEREAS, to obtain for
those persons covered by this Act the maximum benefits allow
able under the Old-Age and Survivors Insurance provisions of
Title II of the Federal Social Security Act, as amended, it will be
necessary for the State Agency to enter into an immediate retro
active agreement with the Federal Security Administrator, and
WHEREAS, upon the execution of such agreement, it will
be necessary, in the proper administration of this Act, to make
immediate deductions from employes' wages.
AN EMERGENCY hereby is declared to exist, and this Act
shall become effective from and after its passage and approval
by the Governor.
Approved March 14, 1951.

CHAPTER 4
(H. R. 1)
A RESOLUTION appropriating additional compensation for officers and
employees of the House and Senate.
WHEREAS, the General Assembly deems the compensation of the offi
cers and employees of the Senate and the House of Representatives,
as provided for in Chapter 88 of the Acts of the General Assembly
of 1936 inadequate for the proper functioning of the business of the
Senate and House of Representatives during the Extraordinary Ses
sion of March, 1951.
NOW THEREFORE,
Be it resolved by the House of Representatives of the Gen
eral Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky:
That there is hereby appropriated from the Legislative Ses
sion Fund as additional compensation for the following officers
and employees of the Senate and House of Representatives: As
sistant Clerk, Enrolling Clerk, Sergeant-at-Arms, Doorkeeper,
Cloakroom Keeper, Janitor and Eages, to that compensation as
provided for in KRS 6.230 the sum of One Hundred Seventy-five
($175.00) Dollars per diem, effective beginning the first day of
the Extraordinary Session of March, 1951, for each body, for
the duration of such Extraordinary Session.
That the aforesaid amount shall be pro-rated among the
aforenamed constitutional officers and employees, according to
the per diem set forth in KRS 6.230, and shall be by the Depart
ment of Finance charged to the account of the aforenamed offi
cers and employees of the Senate and the House of Representa
tives on this basis.
That the Clerk of the Senate and the Clerk of the House of
Representatives are directed to issue vouchers to the Commis
sioner of Finance, who shall draw his warrant in favor of the
Clerk of the Senate and the Clerk of the House of Representa
tives for the above mentioned amounts, and that the Clerk of
the Senate and the Clerk of the House of Representatives are
further directed to distribute the additional compensation to the
above mentioned officers and employees as provided in KRS
6.230.

22 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Whereas, it is necessary that this additional compensation
be received by the officers and employees of the Extraordinary
Session of March, 1951, in order to properly expedite the busi
ness of the Senate and the House of Representatives, an emer
gency is hereby declared to exist and this Resolution shall be ef
fective from and after its passage and approval by the Governor
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky.
Approved March 13, 1951.

CHAPTER 5
(H. R. 2)
A RESOLUTION appropriating the sum of Seven Hundred Fifty ($750.00)
Dollars for a contingent fund for the Clerk of the Senate, and appro
priating the sum of Seven Hundred Fifty ($750.00) Dollars for a con
tingent fund for the Clerk of the House.
Be it ilesolved by the General Assembly of the Common
wealth of Kentucky:
That for the purpose of paying such expenses as the Chief
Clerk of the Senate of Kentucky is directed to incur by the Sen
ate of Kentucky there is set aside out of the appropriation made
by Chapter 38 of the Acts of the 1946 Regular Session for legis
lative sessions as a contingent fund, of Seven Hundred Fifty
Dollars ($750.00); and for the purpose of paying the expenses
incurred by the Chief Clerk of the House of Representatives of
the Commonwealth of Kentucky, there is hereby set aside out
of the appropriation made by Chapter 38 of the Acts of the
1946 Regular Session for legislative sessions as a contingent fund,
the sum of Seven Hundred Fifty Dollars ( $750.00) . Said respec
tive funds shall be paid out on warrant of the Department of
Finance on requisition of the Chief Clerks of the Senate and
of the House of Representatives.
Whereas, it is necessary that these contingent funds for the
Chief Clerks of the Senate and House of Representatives be im
mediately available, an emergency is hereby declared to exist and
this Resolution shall be effective from and after its passage and
approval by the Governor of the Commonwealth of Kentucky.
Approved March 13, 1951.

CHAPTER 6
(H. R. 10)
A RESOLUTION setting aside the sum of Seven Hundred Fifty ($750.00)
Dollars for a contingent fund for the Clerk of the Senate, and setting
aside the sum of Seven Hundred Fifty ($750.00) Dollars for a con
tingent fund for the Clerk of the House, and declaring an emergency.
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Common
wealth of Kentucky:
That for the purpose of paying such expenses as the Chief
Clerk of the Senate of Kentucky is directed to incur by the
Senate of Kentucky there is set aside out of the Legislative Ses
sion Fund as a contingent fund, Seven Hundred Fifty Dollars
($750.00); and for the purpose of p'aying the expenses incurred
by the Chief Clerk of the House of Representatives of the Com
monwealth of Kentucky, there is hereby set aside out of the Leg
islative Session Fund as a contingent fund, th« sum of Seven
Hundred Fifty Dollars ($750.00). Said respective funds shall
be paid out on warrant of the Department of Finance on requisi
tion of the Chief Clerks of the 'Senate and of the House of Repre
sentatives.
Whereas, it is necessary that these contingent funds for the
Chief Clerks of the Senate and House of Representatives be
immediately available, an emergency is hereby declared to exist
and this Resolution shall be effective from and after its passage
and approval by the Governor of the Commonwealth of Ken
tucky.
Approved March 14, 1951.

UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA

CHAPTER 7
(S. R. 6)
A JOINT RESOLUTION of the General Assembly providing for the
interim expenses of the members of the General Assembly, and
declaring an emergency.
WHEREAS, the General Assembly in 1950 enacted House
Bill No. 200 which provided for the payment to each member of
the General Assembly including the LieuterianfcGovernor in his
capacity as 'President of the Senate the sum of ten' dollars per
day for which he draws or has drawn per diem compensation and
declared that said sum was the equivalent of the minimum sum
that each such member has expended or will be required to
expend each day, in the performance of his official duties for tele
phone, telegraph service, supplies and etc., and,
WHEREAS, the General Assembly likewise enacted into law
a bill providing for the payment to the members of the General
Assembly who take office on January 1st, 1952, the sum of twen
ty-five dollars per day; and,
WHEREAS, the members of the present General Assembly
will be declined the provisions of House Bill No. 200 which ap
plied during the regular session of the General Assembly in 1950
and will be declined the sum provided to the new members in
1952; and,
WHEREAS, the members of the General Assembly are en-
titled to receive the same per diem and the expense account of
ten dollars per day which they received during the regular ses1-
sion and which the new members will receive in 1952;
NOW THEREFORE, be it resolved by the Senate and the
House of Representatives that each member of the General As
sembly, including the President of the Senate, during this
Extraordinary Session or any other held during 1951, shall be
paid out of the State Treasury the sum of ten dollars per day
and each day for which he draws compensation during the
period of the present Legislative Session or any other Special
Session, which sum is hereby declared to be the equivalent of the

28 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
minimum sum that each such .member has expended or will be
required to expend each day in the performance of his official
duties, for telephone and telegraph service, supplies, steno
graphic assistance and other miscellaneous assistance. The Com
missioner of Finance which upon the ascertainment of the time
of adjournment of this Extraordinary Session shall draw his war
rant on the State Treasurer in favor of each member of the Gen
eral Assembly, including the President of the Senate, for the
total of the sums payable to each such member under this reso
lution and the necessary amount to pay the same is hereby ap
propriated out of the General Expenditure 'Fund.
The sums so received shall be expended by each member
of the General Assembly in the manner determined by him.
WHEREAS, present economic conditions are imposing an
unconscionable burden, an emergency is declared to exist and
this 'Resolution shall take effect upon its passage and approval
by the Governor.
Approved March 14, 1951.
